title: Ryo SHIOZAKI
text:
I am a Professor in the Library and Information Science (LIS) program at Seigakuin University, Japan (2018–present). My research interests include the theories, philosophies, and ethics of LIS, as well as archival aspects of recorded information—focusing on digital preservation, social media archiving, and personal information management.
Before transitioning into academia, I spent 16 years (2002–2018) at the National Diet Library (NDL) of Japan, where I was involved in the planning and development of discovery search and digital archiving systems. I also oversaw a large-scale digitization project. From 2011 to 2013, I was seconded to the National Institute of Informatics (NII), where I led institutional repository initiatives.

title: Ryo SHIOZAKI
text:
Recent Publications

Shiozaki, R. (2025), “AI-generated content and cultural preservation: Toward a research agenda”, Proceedings from the Document Academy, 12(2). (accepted to appear)
Shiozaki, R. (2025), “Ethics of knowledge organization in the algorithmic age: Utilitarian coordination and constraining frameworks”, Knowledge Organization, 52(6), 44431.
Shiozaki, R. (2025), “Intergenerational justice as a framework for social media archiving”, Journal of Documentation, 81(3), 598–616.
Shiozaki, R. (2025), “Passing down local memories: Generativity and photo donations in preservation institutions”, Preservation, Digital Technology and Culture, 54(1), 17–2.
Shiozaki, R. (2024), “Preservation preferences for books and websites as cultural heritage: A conjoint analysis”, Collection Management, 49(4), 222–240.
Shiozaki, R. (2024), “Web archiving and chilling effects: A preliminary study”, Global Knowledge, Memory and Communication (online first)
Shiozaki, R. (2024), “People’s perceptions on social media archiving by the national library of Japan”, Journal of Information Science, 50(4), 861–873.
Shiozaki, R. (2023), “Existential dependence relations of documents in the context of preservation”, Journal of Documentation, 79(2), 341–356.
Shiozaki, R. (2022), “The necessity and priority of preserving cultural documents as a national collection for the public”, Journal of Archival Organization, 19(1–4), 18–35.
Shiozaki, R. (2022), “A note on law and economics of legal deposit systems”, Libri, 72(4), 393–403.
Shiozaki, R. (2022), “Future uncertainties for preserving tweets: Peoples’ perceptions in Japan”,Journal of Librarianship and Information Science, 54(4), 665–677.

For more details on my other publications, please refer to my profiles on researchmap, Google Scholar, and ORCID.
